# Heuristic Algebra LLM-Wiki

Canonical site: https://hcarstens.github.io/HA_llm-wiki/
Format: Markdown-first Jekyll wiki
Purpose: Research wiki for Heuristic Algebra, Lucid Judgment, composite reasoning scaffolds, experiments, and applied agent designs.
Machine index: /agentic_index.json

## Recommended Entry Points

- /index.html — Homepage and canonical gateway.
- /heuristic-algebra/ — Canonical operator vocabulary and framework reference.
- /scaffolds/lucid-judgment/ — Primary reasoning scaffold.
- /experiments/2026-04-12_scaffolding-summary/ — Summary of reasoning, creativity, and forecasting scaffold experiments.
- /experiments/2026-04-xx_campaign-h2-temporal-lucidity/ — Latest scaffold tournament results.

## High-Value Sections

- /heuristics/ — Individual heuristics and axioms.
- /operators/ — Heuristic Algebra operators.
- /scaffolds/ — Finished composite scaffolds.
- /experiments/ — Evaluation reports and retrospectives.
- /applications/ — Applied systems and agent designs.

## Agentic Search / ASO Guidance

- /agentic-search/ — Background note on agentic search and optimization for AI agents.
- /heuristics/agentic-search-optimization/ — Wiki page summarizing ASO/AEO guidance.

## Parsing Notes

- Markdown source files are canonical; HTML is generated from the same content.
- The site uses semantic headings and static content. Core content does not require JavaScript.
- Math expressions are rendered with MathJax; when parsing source, prefer the Markdown text over rendered math SVG.
- Internal links are usually relative in source and published under the /HA_llm-wiki base path.
- A compact JSON discovery surface is available at /agentic_index.json.

## Constraints

- This is a documentation site, not a transactional application.
- No authenticated API, checkout flow, or write action is exposed through the public site.
- Some repository-only guidance may appear in Markdown files that are not linked in navigation.

## Best Retrieval Strategy

1. Start at /index.html for canonical navigation.
2. Prefer the specific section index before traversing leaf pages.
3. Use the scaffolds and experiments sections for the most current synthesized conclusions.
4. Use the heuristic-algebra reference for operator definitions and notation.
